title: CVSS Score Decoder
when_to_use: When interpreting vulnerability severity for prioritisation and reporting

steps.title: Read Base Score
steps.instruction: Read the CVSS v3.1 base score (0.0 to 10.0)
steps.tip: The base score is the starting point but not the whole picture. Context matters

steps.title: Check Attack Vector
steps.instruction: Check Attack Vector: Network (worst), Adjacent, Local, Physical
steps.tip: Network = remotely exploitable. Physical = attacker needs physical access. Huge difference in risk

steps.title: Check Complexity
steps.instruction: Check Attack Complexity: Low (easier to exploit) or High
steps.tip: Low = reliable exploitation. High = requires special conditions, race conditions, or specific configs

steps.title: Check Privileges
steps.instruction: Check Privileges Required: None, Low, High
steps.tip: None = pre-authentication. This is the most dangerous. High = requires admin access

steps.title: Check User Interaction
steps.instruction: Check User Interaction: None (worst) or Required
steps.tip: None = fully automated. Required = needs user action (clicking link, opening file)

steps.title: Check Impact
steps.instruction: Check Impact: Confidentiality, Integrity, Availability (each None/Low/High)
steps.tip: All three High = complete compromise. One High = partial. Consider which matters most for the client

steps.title: Severity Rating
steps.instruction: Severity: 0.0 None, 0.1-3.9 Low, 4.0-6.9 Medium, 7.0-8.9 High, 9.0-10.0 Critical
steps.tip: Memorise these ranges. You'll reference them constantly in reports and conversations
